verses.text: Issachar's families are reckoned as valiant men of might, tens of thousands strong. A tribe little noted in the great events was full of able men. God's people are not only their famous names but a multitude of faithful, sturdy households.

Issachar's families are reckoned as valiant men of might, tens of thousands strong. A tribe little noted in the great events was full of able men. God's people are not only their famous names but a multitude of faithful, sturdy households.

Benjamin's families are recorded as mighty men of valour. The small tribe, once nearly destroyed, has become strong again. God can revive and multiply what was almost wiped out, raising warriors from a remnant.

Benjamin's families are recorded as mighty men of valour. The small tribe, once nearly destroyed, has become strong again. God can revive and multiply what was almost wiped out, raising warriors from a remnant.
More of Benjamin's clans are numbered as fit for war. The roll honours the tribe's recovered strength. What sin and strife had reduced, God's mercy built up again into a people of valour.
More of Benjamin's clans are numbered as fit for war. The roll honours the tribe's recovered strength. What sin and strife had reduced, God's mercy built up again into a people of valour.

Naphtali's sons are named in a single brief verse, 'the sons of Bilhah.' Even the tribe with the scantiest record is not omitted. God forgets none of His people, however little is written of them.
Manasseh's line is traced through Machir and Gilead. The eastern half-tribe takes its place in the record. God numbers even the families settled far across the Jordan among His own.
Manasseh's line is traced through Machir and Gilead. The eastern half-tribe takes its place in the record. God numbers even the families settled far across the Jordan among His own.
Manasseh's line is traced through Machir and Gilead. The eastern half-tribe takes its place in the record. God numbers even the families settled far across the Jordan among His own.

Among Manasseh's descendants stand the daughters of Zelophehad, whose appeal won them an inheritance. God's record honours the women who sought justice. He upholds the cause of the overlooked and keeps their names.

Among Manasseh's descendants stand the daughters of Zelophehad, whose appeal won them an inheritance. God's record honours the women who sought justice. He upholds the cause of the overlooked and keeps their names.

Among Manasseh's descendants stand the daughters of Zelophehad, whose appeal won them an inheritance. God's record honours the women who sought justice. He upholds the cause of the overlooked and keeps their names.

Ephraim's line is marked by sorrow — sons slain in a cattle raid, the father's long mourning, then a daughter, Sherah, who built three towns. Grief and recovery sit side by side. God brings new life and lasting work even out of a family's deep loss.

Ephraim's line is marked by sorrow — sons slain in a cattle raid, the father's long mourning, then a daughter, Sherah, who built three towns. Grief and recovery sit side by side. God brings new life and lasting work even out of a family's deep loss.
Ephraim's line runs down at last to Joshua, son of Nun. The tribe's grief gives way to its greatest son, the conqueror of Canaan. God brings forth deliverers from families that have known sorrow.

The towns and dwellings of Joseph's sons are listed — Bethel, Shechem, Megiddo, and more. The heartland of Israel is mapped. God settled His people in the land He promised their fathers.
The towns and dwellings of Joseph's sons are listed — Bethel, Shechem, Megiddo, and more. The heartland of Israel is mapped. God settled His people in the land He promised their fathers.
Asher's families begin to be traced, named with their sons and a sister. The far northern tribe is recorded with care. God's account leaves out no branch of His covenant people.

More of Asher's clans are listed in detail. The lesser-known tribe is given its full place. Obscurity before men is no obscurity before God, who numbers each household.

Asher's roll closes, its men called 'choice and mighty men of valour, chief of the princes.' A small, distant tribe is honoured as excellent. God commends faithful strength wherever it is found, not only at the center.
